ground_truth:
OPERATIVE REPORT

Patient: Linda Martinez  DOB: 07/06/1970
MRN: 556789
Date: 22/01/2024
Surgeon: Dr. William Harris, MD, FACS
Assistant: Dr. P. Singh
Anesthesia: General (endotracheal)

Preoperative Diagnosis: Symptomatic cholelithiasis
Postoperative Diagnosis: Same

Procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy

Indications: Recurrent biliary colic. Ultrasound showed multiple
gallstones. Patient failed conservative management.

Description of Procedure:
The patient was placed supine and general anesthesia was induced.
The abdomen was prepped and draped in standard sterile fashion.

A Veress needle was inserted at the umbilicus and pneumoperitoneum
was established to 15 mmHg. A 10mm trocar was placed at the
umbilicus and the laparoscope was inserted, revealing a distended
gallbladder with multiple stones visible through the wall.

Two additional 5mm trocars were placed in the right upper quadrant.
The fundus was retracted superiorly. The infundibulum was identified
and dissected free. The cystic artery and cystic duct were
clipped with three clips each and divided.

The gallbladder was dissected from the liver bed using hook
electrocautery. Hemostasis was achieved. The specimen was placed
in an endoscopic retrieval bag and extracted through the umbilical
port.

The abdomen was desufflated. Ports were removed under direct
visualization. Fascia at the umbilical site was closed with 0-Vicryl.
Skin closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl. Sterile dressings applied.

Estimated Blood Loss: 25 mL
Specimens: Gallbladder with stones (pathology)
Drains: None

Dr. William Harris, MD, FACS
Dictated: 22/01/2024
